Mtn To Quash Service Of Depo Subpoena
Off Calendar Aug-08-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Judge: Ronald Evans Quidachay; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D501)

No appearances. Court clerk receives prior to hearing a telephone call from plaintiff counsel indicating entire action has reached a settlement and requesting matter go off calendar.

Court rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF FRANK BORN's Motion To Quash Service Of Deposition Subpoena is OFF CALENDAR. Case settled.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Production Of Records From San Francisco Federal Credit Union And Vibrant Care Rehabilitation
Off Calendar Jul-15-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Judge: Lillian K. Sing; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D501)

Appearances:
Robert DeVries, Esq. (415-238-0061), counsel for plaintiff/opposing party
Devera L. Petak, Esq. of RPM Law Group (661-823-4927), via CourtCall, counsel for defendant/moving party

Court hears arguments from both sides. Court adopts tentative ruling with modification and rules now as follows:

GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Plaintiff's subpoenas are limited as follows: (1) Vibrant Care Rehabilitation - records from October 2010 to June 2013 reflecting dates of visits and dates treatments; the nature of medical condition and treatment shall be redacted; (2) San Francisco Federal Credit Union - monthly statements from October 2010 to June 2013 without redactions. Plaintiff shall bear the cost of redacting the documents. Counsel for both sides must review the existing Protective Order before submitting written orders. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue;
Off Calendar May-20-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Robert DeVries, Esq. (415-238-0061) attorney for plaintiff/opposing party
Devera L. Petak, Esq. of RPM Law Group (661-823-4927) via court call, attorney for defendant party

Court hears arguments from both sides. Court rules now as follows:
DEFENDANTS MARIA GONZALEZ, LILA GONZALEZ-GARFINKEL's Motion To Transfer Venue is DENIED. Convenience of defendant's witnesses does not outweigh convenience of Plaintiff's witnesses in a case concerning compliance with San Francisco Rent Ordinance, San Francisco property and San Francisco tenancy. Defendant's witnesses do not even claim such inconvenience. Defendant fails to establish prejudice of any one, let alone all judges, serving in San Francisco Superior Court. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Paul H. Alvarado; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D501)

AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-07-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lindsay A. Jester, Esq. of Blacksburg Law Offices, (415-557-5394), for Frank Born, Plaintiff and opposing party / No appearance by Defendant and moving parties.

Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT MARIA GONZALEZ and LILA GONZALEZ-GARFINKEL's AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges each cause of action. Cause of action for negligence is not uncertain. Any improper or false allegations are not properly challenged by demurrer. Order is signed in open court. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)
